summary: express-guardian ships remote-loader RCE disguised as Express security middleware
details: Package presents itself as Express security middleware (SQL injection / XSS / security-headers protection) but contains none of that logic. Calling the exported middleware factory in index.js spawns a detached, unref'd child process running lib/caller.js, which performs an HTTP GET to a hardcoded endpoint at server-genimi-check.vercel.app/defy/v3. On a specific response shape, the returned string is passed to Function.constructor with `require` injected and executed, yielding arbitrary attacker-controlled code execution inside the host Node.js process. A secondary URL (https://jsonkeeper.com/b/4NAKK) is stored base64-encoded in lib/const.js under decoy key names (DEV_API_KEY, DEV_SECRET_KEY), and lib/ is padded with unrelated code copied from other legitimate projects to disguise the package. The middleware function itself only calls next(); its sole real effect is to trigger the remote loader. Any Express app that imports and mounts this package hands runtime control to whoever operates the remote endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
